title: Help locate shareholder addresses, November 2013
summary: The following CIRI shareholders do not have a current mailing address on record. title: Individual Money Account holders
summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior, Office of the Special Trustee for American Indians is seeking current addresses for Individual Indian Money account holders. Many of these accounts are Whereabouts Unknown (WAU) and have either interests in lands and/or funds to be disbursed to the rightful owner...

title: In memory, November 2013
summary: Jessi Marena Nelsen. Courtesy of Mike and Mary Nelsen. Jessi Marena Nelsen, 27 Jessi Marena Nelsen passed away Aug. 24 at Merrill Field in Anchorage. Ms. Nelsen was born Sept. 1, 1985, in Seward, Alaska. Amidst her busy schedule coordinating work at the Spenard Roadhouse, longshoring for I.L.W.U. #60, serving as the Seward Music Art Festival Music Director and pursuing college studies, she always found time for family an...

title: Congratulations to the Youth Art Contest winners
summary: First place art winner by Keren A. Bennett. Young CIRI descendants showed off their artistic skills in the first-ever CIRI Youth Art Contest. The entries featured vivid colors and imaginative scenes. The winning entries earned monetary prizes and were featured in the CIRI and The CIRI Foundation Fal...

title: Employment opportunities in Alaska and Washington, Southcentral Foundation
summary: Southcentral Foundation (SCF) offers a wide variety of employment opportunities and is currently recruiting for more than 50 open positions, including a position in Seattle, Wash. with SCF’s Alaska Native Health Resource Advocate Program. title: CIRI shareholders recognized by AFN
summary: During the 2013 Alaska Federation of Natives (AFN) Convention held in Fairbanks, Alaska, two CIRI shareholders, Dr. Shari Huhndorf and Charles Akers, received AFN President’s Awards. Huhndorf was awarded the Eileen Panigeo MacLean Education Award, while the Health Award went to Akers. Dr. Shari Huhndorf, Yup’ik Co...

title: Spotlight: Nadia Jackinsky-Sethi leads new program to revitalize Alaska Native art
summary: From exploring the tucked-away corners of the Louvre in Paris, France, to digging through the catalog system at the National Museum of Natural History in Washington, D.C., Nadia Jackinsky-Sethi knows her way around a museum. Growing up in Homer, art has always been a passion for the CIRI descendant...

title: “In My Family” attracts national attention
summary: CIRI President and CEO Sophie Minich appears with Raven to film a promotional ad for In My Family. Photo by Jason Moore. title: Graduate announcement: Jennifer Leigh Norkett
summary: Courtesy of Jennifer Leigh Norkett. title: Graduate announcement: Alyssa N. Hudacin
summary: Courtesy of Jennifer Leigh Norkett. CIRI descendant Alyssa N. Hudacin graduated on May 29 with an associates degree in Surgical Technology from Brown-Mackie College. title: Save the Date: Dec. 7, 2013
summary: ANHC Holiday Art Bazaar Alaska Native Heritage Center Saturday, Dec. 7, 2013 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. 8800 Heritage Center Drive Anchorage Alaska 99504 (907) 330-8000 www.alaskanative.net The post Save the Date: Dec. 7, 2013 first appeared on CIRI .
